notes: Pi natively reopens a prior session at relaunch (`pi -c` continue-last / `pi -r` / `pi --session <transcript>`), reusing the SAME <ts>_<uuid>.jsonl under ~/.pi/agent/sessions/--<cwd>--/. Because the adapter watches that directory (FilesUnderRoot), the resumed process appends to the existing transcript and the daemon sees the SAME session_id row come back after the first lifetime exits — exactly the claudecode/codex shape: ONE session id, TWO PIDs. v1_session_birth matches the pre-session (proc-<PID>) entering ready at launch; the turn drives the UUID-keyed transcript session, so the turn/exit/resume phases pin to v1_turn_start (the real transcript session) via same_session_as — NOT to v1_session_birth (the pre-session), which never goes working and never reappears after exit. The resumed half re-binds the SAME transcript session id (not a new one), then a second turn drives ready→working→ready on it. Recorded with the pi interactive driver's exit_clean+resume primitives (commit e88e35b1): the resume step Ctrl-D's the running pi (→ process_exited), then relaunches `pi --session <transcript>` in the same cwd so DiscoverPID re-binds the new PID to the existing session.

text: First launch: session appears in ready
First user prompt drives ready → working on the transcript session
First turn completes back to ready
Clean exit (Ctrl-D) after the first turn: process_exited for the transcript session

Spec: 'Same session_id reappears (not a new session row)' — the resumed pi reopens the SAME <ts>_<uuid>.jsonl, so the SAME session id re-binds to a new PID in ready. Generous 60s bound: pi turns are slow, and this measures from the FIRST-lifetime process_exited through the resume relaunch and the first re-bind ready emission, so the window must absorb pi's relaunch + settle latency.
Spec: 'ready → working on the first new turn' — the second turn drives the SAME transcript session to working
Second turn completes back to ready on the SAME session — each PID lifetime's arc is independently correct
